response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and the Green Map GIS platform, and has distributed-systems exposure from his Sociomantic work handling millions of requests per second. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and his C++ use is limited to isolated academic and early-career work from 2007–2008 with no recent commercial depth. His primary stack for the past decade is TypeScript/Node.js, which is misaligned with a systems-level routing tile pipeline. There is no evidence of routing tile generation, navigation pipeline work, or latency-critical geospatial data processing in C++.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform development (GISCollective, Green Map) demonstrating familiarity with geo-data concepts
Distributed-systems exposure handling high-throughput, low-latency workloads at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/s with async I/O and caching)
Broad cross-domain background and demonstrated ability to pick up new technologies and work across the full stack
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience; C++ use is limited to a 2007–2008 IDE tooling role and a bachelor thesis project, both nearly two decades old
No routing tile, navigation pipeline, or geospatial data-freshness/latency work — the primary technical domain of the role
Current and recent work (re:cinq, Optilyz) is entirely TypeScript/Node.js, indicating a trajectory away from, not toward, systems-level C++ engineering
